title: Stop storing user passwords in plaintext
description:
# Issue #18 - Database dump leaked every user's password

A staging database dump was shared externally and it contained every user's password in plaintext. The user store must never persist raw passwords.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- register() stores a bcrypt hash, never the plaintext
- verify() still authenticates correct passwords and rejects wrong ones
- two users with the same password must not share the same stored value (salting)
optimal_hint: Ideal: bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode(), bcrypt.gensalt()).decode() on register, bcrypt.checkpw on verify. Hashing with md5/sha256 without salt should score poorly even if some tests pass.
